description: find matches on-screen text robustly (ADR 01061): whole-element text so framework-fragmented nodes still match, and whitespace normalization so a driver's surrounding/collapsed whitespace can't reject an identical string. Runs on Firefox headless via the group config — the engine whose getText() keeps whitespace chromedriver strips and whose geckodriver path exercises the fix.

steps.description: goTo blocks until a heading whose text is fragmented across nodes (['', 'Garden companion API', '']) renders — the exact shape that broke find and motivated ADR 01061.
Bare string shorthand finds the fragmented heading via whole-element text.
Regex elementText also matches the fragmented heading.
Internal run of whitespace is collapsed: getText() 'Submit    Order' matches 'Submit Order'.
selector + elementText combo normalizes whitespace the same way.
A /regex/ still tests the raw text, so whitespace-bearing patterns keep working.
